content.problemStatement:
A pair of arrays AAA and BBB, both of length NNN, are called perfect if it is possible to form an array CCC of length NNN satisfying the following conditions:


Ci=AiC_i = A_iCi​=Ai​ or Ci=BiC_i = B_iCi​=Bi​ for each 1≤i≤N1 \le i \le N1≤i≤N
Ci<Ci+1C_i < C_{i + 1}Ci​<Ci+1​ for each 1≤i<N1 \le i < N1≤i<N
You are given 222 arrays AAA and BBB of length NNN. It is guaranteed that each element from 111 to 2⋅N2 \cdot N2⋅N either occurs once in AAA or occurs once in BBB.

Find how many pairs LLL and RRR exist such that:


1≤L≤R≤N1 \le L \le R \le N1≤L≤R≤N
The pair of arrays A[L,R]A[L, R]A[L,R] and B[L,R]B[L, R]B[L,R] are perfect.
Here, A[L,R]A[L, R]A[L,R] represents the subrange [AL,AL+1,…,AR][A_L, A_{L + 1}, \ldots, A_R][AL​,AL+1​,…,AR​] of the array AAA.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of input contains a single integer NNN - the size of the arrays.
The second line contains NNN integers - A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.
The third line contains NNN integers - B1,B2,…,BNB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_NB1​,B2​,…,BN​.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the number of LLL and RRR, such that corresponding subranges in AAA and BBB are perfect.
constraints: Ai≠Aj,Bi≠BjA_i \ne A_j, B_i \ne B_jAi​=Aj​,Bi​=Bj​ for any 1≤i<j≤N1 \le i \lt j \le N1≤i<j≤N
Ai≠BjA_i \ne B_jAi​=Bj​ for any 1≤i,j≤N1 \le i, j \le N1≤i,j≤N
The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : All the pairs of LLL and RRR (satisfying 1≤L≤R≤N1 \le L \le R \le N1≤L≤R≤N) are perfect except L=1,R=3L = 1, R = 3L=1,R=3. For example, for L=1,R=2L = 1, R = 2L=1,R=2, we can choose C=[3,6]C = [3, 6]C=[3,6].
Test Case 2 : All the pairs LLL and RRR are perfect. For example, for L=1,R=3L = 1, R = 3L=1,R=3, we can choose C=[2,5,6]C = [2, 5, 6]C=[2,5,6].
